column1: # YouTube source clips for words missing from WLASL.
# Format (TAB-separated):
#   gloss   url   signer_tag   start_sec   end_sec
# Tips for finding good clips:
#   - Search "ASL <word>" on YouTube.
#   - Best channels (clear single-word demos, single signer, frontal view):
#       * Bill Vicars / Lifeprint        — search "Bill Vicars <word>"
#       * Dr. Bill Vicars (ASL University)
#       * Signing Savvy                  — most have free public previews
#       * Signed With Heart
#       * ASL Meredith
#   - signer_tag: use the channel name in lowercase, no spaces (e.g. lifeprint,
#     signingsavvy, aslmeredith). The extractor splits by signer_tag so the
#     same signer never appears in both train and test.
#   - start/end in seconds. Watch the video; pick a ~2-3 second window that
#     covers just the sign itself (no intro / outro / repetition).
#   - Aim for 5-10 clips per gloss across at least 2-3 different signer_tags.
# Lines starting with # are ignored. Delete this header block when you start
# adding real URLs, or keep it — the extractor skips comment lines either way.
# Example rows (replace with real URLs once you've sourced them):
# hello
# hello
# thank_you
